ai_overview: NOTE: Viktor's Stakeholder Digest email could not be retrieved — Microsoft 365 authentication is pending and this digest is built from Slack and Jira sources only. Sunday 26 April was a low-activity day across the board, consistent with a weekend. Two GDCU Jira tickets were updated: GDCU-8512 (Always On Betting Tips Page, assigned to Miguel Migneco, In Product) and GDCU-8919 (a new related task, auto-created, in Backlog). From the preceding week's Slack activity, the headline story is Ciara Carroll publishing the gambling.com design system to Claude Design — 30+ components, 12+ elements, full icon set, and brand identity — a significant platform milestone before she goes on leave Monday. Colin Brannigan was the most active senior stakeholder on Friday, discussing AI product strategy and a potential product engineer hire. Vic Dadson remains on two-week sick leave (signed off 21 April). Chloe Christie and Josh Smith are managing DC8 cycle planning, including a new pre-project checklist initiative. Harden Wallace provided a thorough technical response on Cosmo Wheel single-winner mechanics. No blockers or hotfixes flagged over the weekend.

people.name: Vic Dadson
people.summary: Remains on two-week medical sick leave signed off by her doctor on 21 April. She messaged Mike directly about her condition and a family bereavement. HR and Bob have been notified. No Jira or Slack activity on 26 April. She had previously been making progress on data/analytics work before her absence.

people.name: Jessica Dordevic
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April (Sunday). Most recent activity was 24 April: she ran a usability test for the Tinder Widget (GDCU-8546) and shared Figma findings with Mike, flagging several UX considerations. On 22 April she took a sick day but still had a QA call about login issues on the IUJ and sent Tinder design options to Mike and Ciara. Currently her primary focus is the Tinder-Style Offer Match widget design.

people.name: Miguel Migneco
people.summary: GDCU-8512 (Always On Betting Tips Page) was updated in Jira on 26 April — Miguel is the assignee and the ticket is In Product status, indicating design review is underway. On 23 April he shared the finalised Figma for the Betting Tips Hub with Mike and had a quick sync about Ace. Earlier in the week he was active in #gdc-product-led-growth and #gdc-product-design-pod discussing close icon consistency, NZ header logos, and the Jump Links / Anchor Menu component (GDCU-8799).

people.name: Ciara Carroll
people.summary: No activity on 26 April — she is going on leave from Monday 28 April. Before leave she had a highly active week: on 24 April she published the gambling.com design system to Claude Design (30+ components, 12+ elements, 300+ icons, 200+ images, full brand/colour/typography tokens, and Orion Git integration) — a significant team milestone. She also coordinated the Bet365 T&C solution with Mike on the same day. Note: she informed Mike she will be on leave Monday.

people.name: Josh Smith
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April (Sunday). Most recent Slack activity was 22 April in DMs with Mike, discussing Clarity session recordings and filtering bot traffic from analytics. He posted DC7 full release notes and DC8 high-priority planned work to #gdc-core on 17 April. Currently managing DC8 priorities including Global HP Geolocation of Images, Image Carousel Shortcode, and the FTP App build.

people.name: Chloe Christie
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April (Sunday). Throughout the week she was the most consistently active PO: posted release ticket lists in #6amigos (22 Apr), posted the non-en sync agenda (21 Apr), coordinated design pod discussions, and collaborated with Mike on a new pre-project requirements checklist covering 10+ standard questions for new features. Currently managing DC8 sprint planning and capacity across design and dev.

people.name: Carlos Blauth
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April (Sunday). Last meaningful DM with Mike was 9 April regarding Ace AI feedback report volumes (confirming one production report per day is correct). Was named as a key contributor to the Ace launch in March. Currently involved in the FTP App and deep-link betslip project based on channel membership.

people.name: Eoin McCarthy
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April (Sunday). Most recent Slack activity was 22 April in a DM with Mike, providing a clear technical assessment of page size concerns for the Betting Tips Hub load-more pagination — he confirmed that loading ~30 tips should be manageable given the Matrix API. Currently overseeing the high-agency dev approach and is a member of the FTP App and deep-link betslip channels.

people.name: Harden Wallace
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April (Sunday). Most recent Slack activity was 23–24 April: responded to Mike's question about manipulating the Cosmo Wheel to guarantee the next UK user wins the iPhone. Harden gave a thorough technical explanation covering why the current RNG system cannot support a single-winner lock, and recommended raising a proper ticket rather than using a manual workaround. Mike acknowledged and will investigate options.

people.name: Kelvin Yee
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April (Sunday). Last DM with Mike was 9 April, discussing filtering stale racing tips from the news hub Most Popular section using article publish timestamps and tagging logic. He proposed using Site Article Categories with a 'Tips' tag as the filtering mechanism. No activity visible in the past week in tracked channels.

people.name: Milan Nikolic
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April. Last DM with Mike was 26 March regarding the Global Strategy Hub (GDCU-8653), specifically whether to serve the strategy page in English with localised casino results. In #gdc-high-agency on 13 March he flagged that 5pm meetings are too late for Serbia. No Jira assignments or Slack posts found for the past week.

people.name: Nebojsa Stanic
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April. Last DM with Mike was December 2025, demonstrating a Tinder-style casino card prototype for the AI Day session. No Jira assignments or Slack posts found in recent channels. No activity visible in any tracked channel in 2026.

people.name: Novica Vukobratovic
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April. Last DM with Mike was February 2026 regarding a Cosmo Spins reskin coordination issue. In #gdc-deep-link-betslip-project (April 7) he was mentioned by Chloe as the source of guidance on using square logo variants for NZ headers. No activity visible in tracked channels in April 2026.

people.name: Sanja Budic
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April (Sunday). Most recent Slack activity was 8 April in #gdc-deep-link-betslip-project, where she reported that the /api/mcp/deep_links endpoint for the Bookies brand was returning responses above 5 seconds — a performance concern flagged to Alex Kornilov. She was named as a key contributor to the Ace AI launch by Mike in #gdc-core. Last DM with Mike was 7 April.

people.name: Robin John
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April. DM history with Mike shows no messages. In #gdc-high-agency they joined the channel on 6 March. No Jira tickets or Slack messages attributable to Robin found in the past week of tracked channels.

people.name: Shane McGrath
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April (Sunday). Most recent Slack activity visible was in #prism-cms-feedback (March 30–31) where he filed multiple detailed QA bug reports: duplicate taxonomy causing 500 error, inability to rename image files on frontend, past-date drag-and-drop scheduling, and a change sets delete error. In #gdc-learning-hub (March 6) he reported blurry graph rendering and insufficient time for feedback messages in the Speed Round. Active QA contributor to Prism CMS and Learning Hub testing.

people.name: Dean Ryan
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April (Sunday). Most recent DM with Mike was 24 April discussing the Stable Stars page — Dean asked when a dedicated page would be live and Mike confirmed early-to-mid May, targeting before Epsom (missing Guineas). Also asked about managing Cosmo Wheel iPhone prize winners (confirmed 3 of the current winners were internal). Previously in #gdc-f2p-apps (15 Apr) suggested the app be restructured as a Cosmo-branded slots app.

people.name: Michael Butlin
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April (Sunday). Most recent DM with Mike was 24 April: Mike asked Michael to complete a 5-minute stakeholder form reviewing the Tinder Widget design (GDCU-8546), and Michael also flagged that the Bet365 T&C scrollable fix on qa10 still doesn't resolve the grid layout case. Active stakeholder review and feedback in the preceding week.

people.name: Dan Smullen
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April (Sunday). Last DM with Mike was March 5 regarding the new Strategy Hub sign-off. In #gdc-f2p-apps (15 Apr) he reported F2P game issues and the Cosmo Spins 24-hour wait bug after login. In #core-division-leads (15 Apr) Colin asked Dan about Accuranker access for BF and OGX reports. No recent Slack or Jira activity visible.

people.name: Gabriel Cornoiu
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April (Sunday). Most recent DM with Mike was 23 April, where Gabriel contributed to the pre-project checklist initiative by suggesting two additional standard questions: SEO and information architecture considerations for new pages/hubs, and which operator offer attributes should be displayed. In #6amigos (22 Apr) he raised the CA/FR Oplist Redesign for the next sprint. Previously active on Prism CMS fixes and the F2P app feedback channel setup.

people.name: Diana Thurmann
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April (Sunday). Most recent DM with Mike was 22 April when Mike shared a Clarity API key and usage instructions (10 calls/day limit). She created the new #gdc-nonen-market-updates channel on 21 April for daily industry updates across non-EN markets. Previously announced the Iceland geo launch in #core-division on 16 April, crediting the cross-team effort.

people.name: Colin Brannigan
people.summary: No observable activity on 26 April (Sunday). Most recent DM with Mike was 24 April — an active strategic conversation discussing Claude Design concepts (Colin shared a wireframe he built), the product engineer role candidate, and alignment on shipping product faster without 50 design iterations pre-launch. In #core-division-leads (24 Apr) he nudged teams to share wins in #core-division and discussed NZ regulatory concerns around affiliate marketing restrictions. Active and engaged on AI product strategy.
